content_summary: Describes dissociation of mouse brain tissue and single-cell suspension preparation used for scRNA-seq library generation.

location: Section 2.4 - Nuclei Isolation and Library Preparation for snATAC-Seq
content_summary: Describes nuclei isolation from two cohorts of APP/PS1 mice and library preparation using 10x Genomics Chromium Next GEM Single Cell ATAC Kit v2. Sequenced on Illumina NovaSeq S2 PE100 targeting at least 10000 nuclei per sample.

location: Section 2.5 - Chromatin Immunoprecipitation and Sequencing - ChIP-Seq
content_summary: Describes ChIP-seq protocol using anti-RXR antibody on brain lysates from Bexarotene- and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice (n=2 per group). Libraries sequenced on Illumina HiSeq2000.

Describes reanalysis of scRNA-seq data generated by Saibro-Girardi et al. using Cell Ranger v7.0 and Seurat. 37634 cells retained after filtering. Clustering at 0.25 resolution and differential expression using MAST algorithm.

location: Section 2.6.2 - snATAC-Seq Data Processing and Cell Type Annotation
content_summary: Describes snATAC-seq processing using cellranger-atac count and ArchR. Quality filtering yielded high-quality nuclei. Cluster identities assigned via unconstrained integration with scRNA-seq. Peaks called with MACS2 and peak-to-gene links identified.

location: Section 2.6.3 - Identification of Candidate TF Regulators
content_summary: ChromVAR used to predict TF activity enrichment per cell from snATAC-seq. Pearson correlation between motif enrichment Z-scores and ATAC-inferred gene expression (FDR < 0.01 and correlation > 0.5) and also with RNA expression from scRNA-seq.

location: Section 2.6.4 - Analysis of Differentially Accessible Peaks
content_summary: Differentially accessible regions between Bexarotene and Vehicle identified using Signac FindMarkers with logistic regression. Functional annotation with DAVID.

location: Section 2.6.5 - Transcription Factor Footprinting and Regulatory Networks
content_summary: TOBIAS pipeline applied to snATAC-seq differentially accessible peaks for TF footprinting. BINDetect identified differentially bound TFs between conditions. TF-TF regulatory network constructed using CreateNetwork module and visualized in Cytoscape.

location: Section 2.6.6 - ChIP-Seq Analysis and Validation of snATAC-Seq Data
content_summary: ChIP-seq reads aligned with Bowtie2 to GRCm38. MACS2 peak calling comparing Bexarotene vs Vehicle (q-value 0.05). Overlapping regions between ChIP-seq and ATAC-seq determined by Bedtools with 10 kb windows. Overlapping peaks annotated by Uropa and scanned for RXR heterodimer motifs using MEME Suite FIMO. Metascape enrichment analysis performed.

location: Section 3.1 - Single-Cell RNA and ATAC Profiling Identify Major Cell Types in APP/PS1 Mouse Brain
content_summary: Reports profiling of 5-month-old APP/PS1 mouse brains yielding 61353 nuclei (snATAC-seq) and 37640 cells (scRNA-seq). Unsupervised clustering and UMAP identified major brain cell populations. Marker gene analysis confirmed cluster identity.
Experimental design schematic showing APP/PS1 mice treated with Bexarotene or Vehicle for snATAC-seq (n=5 per group) and ChIP-seq.
UMAP projections of integrated scRNA-seq and snATAC-seq datasets showing sample treatment distribution and cell type annotations across 61353 nuclei.
Bar chart showing proportions of annotated peak distributions (distal/exonic/intronic/promoter) across major cell-type groups from snATAC-seq (n=5 per group).
Heatmap of normalized expression of top marker genes (Aqp4/Prlr/Pglyrp1/Pf4/Tmem119/Slc17a7/Cldn11/Vtn) for each cell type from scRNA-seq.

Coverage plots showing chromatin accessibility across major cell types for representative marker genes. Bottom panels show peak-to-gene linkage analysis correlating peak accessibility and gene activity.

location: Section 3.2 - RXR Activation Remodels Chromatin Accessibility and Prioritizes Upstream Regulators
content_summary: ChromVAR motif enrichment across major cell types from snATAC-seq identifies 88 high-confidence candidate TF regulators (Pearson > 0.5 and FDR < 0.01). Examples include Pax6 (astrocytes) and Lef1 (endothelium) and Neurod6 (neurons) and Runx3 (macrophages) and Mafb (microglia) and Nfe2l3 (oligodendrocytes).

Heatmaps of 88 candidate TF regulators shown as chromVAR motif enrichment (A) and ATAC-inferred gene expression from ArchR (B) and RNA expression from scRNA-seq (C) across cell types. Side panels show Pearson correlations between ATAC-inferred TF expression and chromVAR or gene expression.

UMAP embeddings of example candidate TF regulators (Pax6/Lef1/Neurod6/Runx3/Mafb/Nfe2l3) showing motif enrichment (D) and inferred gene expression from ATAC-seq (E) and gene expression from scRNA-seq (F).

location: Section 3.3 - Integration of scRNA-Seq and snATAC-Seq Data
content_summary: Reports integration of scRNA-seq and snATAC-seq using feature linkages to map cell-type and condition-specific cis-regulatory elements. 1605 peaks more accessible with RXR activation and 1310 enriched in Vehicle. Differentially accessible peaks concentrated in microglia and endothelial cells.

Heatmap of differentially accessible peaks (left: 1310 down / 14170 ns / 1685 up) and linked genes (right) between RXR-activated and Vehicle groups across major cell types. Z-scores shown for both accessibility and expression levels.

Coverage plots of differentially accessible peaks at representative loci (Apoe/Fkbp5 in microglia; Hes1/Sik1 in endothelial cells; Nupr1 in astrocytes; Pdgfra in oligodendrocytes) showing peak coordinates and genomic links and expression levels under each condition.

GO enrichment analysis of biological processes associated with upregulated genes (left) and downregulated genes (right) in microglia following RXR activation. Upregulated: cell migration / response to hypoxia / inflammatory response / HDL remodeling. Downregulated: chromatin remodeling / protein phosphorylation / nervous system development.

GO enrichment analysis of biological processes associated with upregulated (left) and downregulated (right) genes in endothelial cells following RXR activation. Upregulated: angiogenesis / cell migration / TGF-beta signaling. Downregulated: transcription regulation / apoptotic process / insulin secretion.

In microglia 1752 differential peaks identified (856 up / 816 down). RXR activation increased accessibility affecting cell migration (Ccr1) / cell differentiation (Dusp6) / inflammatory response (Cx3cr1) / phosphorylation (Bmp2k / Cbl) / HDL remodeling (Apoe / Lipg / Lipc / Lpl). Apoe locus peaks significantly upregulated (log2FC = 1.66). Fkbp5 peaks significantly downregulated (log2FC = -0.94).

Endothelial cells showed 1717 differential peaks (789 up / 928 down). Hes1 peak upregulated (log2FC = 0.70) with mRNA upregulation (log2FC = 0.38). Sik1 peak downregulated (log2FC = -0.29) with mRNA downregulation (log2FC = -0.14). Upregulated GO categories include angiogenesis and cell migration.
276 differential peaks in astrocytes (26 up / 252 down). Nupr1 showed increased chromatin accessibility (log2FC = 0.52) associated with increased expression (mRNA log2FC = 0.14).

185 differential peaks in oligodendrocytes (64 up / 121 down). Pdgfra upregulated after treatment (mRNA log2FC = 0.27) matching increased chromatin accessibility (log2FC = 0.72). Mertk also showed increased expression (mRNA log2FC = 0.11) with increased accessibility (log2FC = 0.51).
Relatively few differential peaks in neurons (110 total). GO terms associated with dendrite morphogenesis / synaptic transmission / regulation of synaptic plasticity.

location: Section 3.4 - Transcription Factor Footprinting Reveals RXR-Centered Regulatory Circuits
content_summary: TOBIAS footprinting analysis on open chromatin profiles from astrocytes / endothelial cells / oligodendrocytes / microglia identifies differentially bound TFs between RXR-activated and Vehicle groups. Some TFs (Ets1 / Erg / Gabpa) showed stronger binding in Vehicle across all cell types. 20-27% of TFs showed concordant footprinting and differential expression changes.

location: Section 3.4 - TF footprinting with expression integration
content_summary: Comparing footprinting changes with differential expression (scRNA-seq) showed 20-27% of TFs with consistent regulation at both levels. Olig2 had higher footprinting and expression in astrocytes and oligodendrocytes. Representative altered footprints: Alx1 in astrocytes / Erg in endothelial cells / Spi1 in microglia / Neurog1 in oligodendrocytes.

TOBIAS network module predicted a cascade of 25 primary TFs activated by ligand-bound RXR that collectively activate 141 additional TFs. Reveals an RXR-centered regulatory hierarchy across brain cell types.
Schematic workflow for TF footprinting using TOBIAS and regulatory network construction from snATAC-seq data.

Heatmap of differentially bound TFs between RXR-activated and Vehicle groups in astrocytes / endothelial cells / microglia / oligodendrocytes. Red = higher binding in RXR activation; blue = higher in Vehicle.

Bar charts showing representative TF occupancy (white) and gene expression changes (gray; log2FC Bexarotene vs Vehicle). Donut plots show percentage of TFs with concordant occupancy and expression changes (20-27%).

Representative TF footprints with significantly altered occupancy (FDR < 0.05): Alx1 in astrocytes / Erg in endothelial cells / Spi1 in microglia / Neurog1 in oligodendrocytes. Red = Bexarotene-corrected signals; blue = Vehicle.
RXR-centered TF regulatory network. Circles = direct RXR targets; octagons = indirect TFs downstream of direct targets. Node color = mean log2FC in binding score; node size = TFBS score.

Neuronal TF regulatory network showing TFs with significantly different binding scores (Bexarotene vs Vehicle). Colored circles = significantly altered TFs; gray circles = direct TF targets. Circle size = number of direct TF targets; color = mean log2FC in binding score.

location: Section 3.5 - Differential Regulatory Networks in Neuronal Systems
content_summary: TF regulatory networks reconstructed from footprinting in neurons. Networks compared between RXR-activated and Vehicle groups. 134 TFs and thousands of edges with comparable size / density (~0.18-0.20) / and global connectivity. Core universal regulators (Klf4 / Egr2 / Zfx / Foxn1 / Nrf1) maintained highest out-degrees. RXR activation enhanced Atoh1 -> Neurod2 cascade.

location: Section 3.6 - ChIP-Seq Validation of RXR-Bound Regulatory Elements
content_summary: RXR ChIP-seq identified 11957 significant binding peaks. 3908 overlapped with enriched ATAC-seq peaks. Among 1251 overlapping peaks 777 at TSSs and 60 in upstream promoter regions (~70% proximal CREs). GO and pathway analysis of overlapping peaks showed enrichment in neurodegenerative disease KEGG pathways (AD / PD / HD / PrD / ALS).
Venn-style bar chart showing unique and overlapping peaks between ATAC-seq (2657 unique / 1251 overlapping) and ChIP-seq (total 3908 ATAC peaks involved).
Bar chart showing total ChIP-seq peaks: 10712 ChIP-unique peaks and 1245 overlapping peaks out of 11957 total RXR-binding peaks.
Distribution of 1251 overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peaks by relative position: 777 at TSSs and 60 upstream and additional at other genomic features.

location: Section 3.7 - Integrative Single-Cell Analysis Links RXR Activation to Candidate Cis-Regulatory Elements
content_summary: Integrated analysis of overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peaks within ENCODE regulatory signatures identified seven TFs of particular interest: Myc / Arid5a / Creb3l2 / Egr1 / Zic2 / Gli2 / Nfatc2. Each showed cell-type-specific expression and regulatory associations. Only Myc and Nfatc2 had previously recognized direct regulatory input from retinoid receptors. RXR/Nfatc2 co-binding significantly altered by RXR activation.

content_summary: Circos plot visualizing overlap among gene lists from RXR ChIP-seq binding and ATAC-seq motif enrichment. Outer arcs = gene lists; inner arcs = member genes.

Network of enriched pathways associated with genes overlapping ATAC-seq and ChIP-seq peaks. Includes connective tissue / organelle organization / phosphorylation / mitochondrial organization / Hippo signaling / angiogenesis / DNA repair / neurodegenerative disorders / vesicle-mediated transport.
Table of TF names / JASPAR RXRalpha (PPARgamma::RXRalpha) motifs / coordinates within overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peaks / and ENCODE candidate CRE signatures.
Heatmap of log2FC expression for genes near overlapping ATAC-seq and ChIP-seq peaks implicated in neurodegenerative disorders (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
dataset_source: GEO: GSE283906; GEO: GSE289039; GEO: GSE283905

content_summary: Cell-type-specific expression of TFs located near overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peaks across oligodendrocytes / microglia / astrocytes / endothelial cells.
dataset_source: GEO: GSE283906; GEO: GSE289039; GEO: GSE283905

content_summary: Explicitly states snATAC-seq and ChIP-seq data deposited at GEO under GSE283906 and GSE289039. scRNA-seq data under GSE283905 reanalyzed for integrative purposes.

Quality control metrics for scRNA-seq and snATAC-seq. Panels A-D: violin plots of molecule counts / gene counts / mitochondrial percentage for scRNA-seq. Panel E: TSS enrichment and fragment distributions for snATAC-seq. Panel F: ArchR doublet enrichment for snATAC-seq. Panel G: Distribution of cells/nuclei between treatment groups across cell types.

Panel A: Dot plot of log2FC distribution of differentially accessible peaks between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups in different cell types. Panels B-C: GO enrichment of differentially accessible peaks and expressed genes in astrocytes (B) and oligodendrocytes (C).

Differential TF binding detection in major cell types using TOBIAS-BINDetect. Violin plots of differentially bound TFs in Bexarotene vs Vehicle for astrocytes (A) / endothelial cells (B) / microglia (C) / oligodendrocytes (D) / neurons (E) / and by sample level (F).
ChromVAR computed motif enrichment / inferred gene expression / gene expression from scRNA-seq / and their correlations for the 88 cell-type-specific candidate TFs. Related to Figure 2.
Differentially accessible peak list between Bexarotene- and Vehicle-treated groups and GO terms associated with DA peaks across various cell types. Related to Figure 3.
Bexarotene versus Vehicle differentially bound TF analysis using TOBIAS footprinting. Related to Figure 4.
Overlapping peaks where RXRalpha binds in both ATAC-seq and ChIP-seq datasets with corresponding annotations. Related to Figure 5.
